title: Astronomers Just Found a Milky Way “Fossil” That Survived 12.5 Billion Years Hidden Deep in Our Galaxy
description: Hidden deep within the Milky Way, a remarkable object has quietly preserved traces of the galaxy’s ancient past. New observations are finally revealing just how unusual it really is.

title: Finance Stalls Amid Fossil Fuel Confusion at Bonn Talks
description: Bonn, Germany – The Bonn Climate Change Conference has ended with important work still to be done to progress efforts to phase-out fossil fuels

title: Scientists discover radioactive stardust still falling on Earth from an ancient cosmic blast
description: Scientists often look to distant galaxies for answers, but sometimes those clues are hidden beneath Earth’s oceans. In a stunning discovery, researchers found radioactive stardust from an ancient cosmic explosion still reaching our planet today. Tiny traces of plutonium preserved in deep-sea crust reveal a story that began millions of years ago. The finding shows that Earth remains connected to powerful events unfolding across the universe. It also offers fresh insight into how the elements around us were first created.

title: Ancient Secrets Unveiled: Fossils in Odisha Reveal Baripada's Marine Past
description: Recent fossil discoveries in Baripada, Odisha, have unveiled evidence of a once-submerged marine environment during the Miocene epoch. Researchers, led by Dr. Debabrata Nandi, have documented fossilized shark teeth and molluscan shells, dating back millions of years. This significant find not only sheds light on the region's prehistoric biodiversity but also raises questions about geological changes, including the coastline's retreat. The Baripada Fossil Bed is now recognized as a crucial geological archive, prompting calls for its preservation as a geo-heritage site. Explore the fascinating insights these fossils provide into ancient ocean ecosystems and climatic shifts.

title: Ancient Teeth from Siberia Rewrite the Plague’s Timeline, Dating Back to over 5,500 Years Ago
description: Scientists have found the oldest known evidence of the plague, which sparked deadly outbreaks dating back about 5,500 years ago — some 200 years earlier than previously thought. The disease has sickened humans for thousands of years and wiped out a significant chunk of Europe's population in the 14th century during what's known as the Black Death. Though rare, the plague is still around today and is treated with antibiotics.
